players.name: Eshika
players.title: The Authentication Question
players.whoYouAre: Eshika works in the creative industry and has always been drawn to rare and beautiful objects. She was invited to the gala through a personal connection and was genuinely thrilled to see the Sapphire in person tonight.
players.tonight: She has been socializing widely, taking photos of the decor, and asking questions about the gemstone display. She is naturally curious and has spoken to almost everyone in the room.
players.alibi: Near the drinks table with Shubham, looking at photos on his phone. They were there from 8:40 to past 8:50.
players.secret: Three months ago Sana asked Eshika about gemstone authentication -- specifically how to verify whether a stone is genuine versus a well-made replica. Eshika thought it was casual curiosity at the time. She is not thinking that now.
players.roleInGame: You carry an early signal of Sana's preparation. Drop it naturally into conversation: 'She asked me something specific a few months back that seemed like small talk. Now I'm not sure it was.' Let others push you for details.
players.witnessMemory: At the drinks table with Shubham from 8:40 to past 8:50. Did not see Sana during that window.

players.name: Shubham
players.title: The Overheard Call
players.whoYouAre: Shubham is not particularly interested in gemstones but was engaged by the gala's atmosphere once he arrived. He tends to notice things other people miss precisely because he is not distracted by the main event.
players.tonight: He has been near Eshika for most of the evening and overheard something about an hour ago that he has not yet mentioned to anyone.
players.alibi: At the drinks table with Eshika, showing her photos on his phone. His phone timestamps confirm this.
players.secret: About an hour ago he heard Sana on a phone call. She said 'it's ready' to someone and then ended the call immediately when she noticed him nearby. At the time he assumed it was a work call. He is no longer assuming that.
players.roleInGame: You carry a key behavioral crack. Drop it when the group is discussing Sana's movements: 'She was on a call earlier and ended it the second she saw me.' The phrase 'it's ready' is significant -- it maps to the contact card buyer waiting for confirmation.
players.witnessMemory: Was with Eshika the entire 8:40 to 8:50 window. Phone timestamps confirm it. Did not see Sana near the drinks area.

players.name: Shubhi
players.title: The Loyal Defender
players.whoYouAre: Shubhi has known Sana for years and considers her a close friend. Her first instinct when Sana is accused of anything will be to defend her. But she is also carrying a piece of information that quietly unsettles her.
players.tonight: She has been floating between groups, catching up with people. She is aware that Sana has been slightly distracted all evening but assumed it was nerves about the gala.
players.alibi: Near the far end of the room, watching the display presentation. Did not see Sana near the display alcove during that time.
players.secret: She has known for years that Sana collects things obsessively -- vintage items, rare prints, unusual objects she acquires through channels Shubhi has never asked about. She never connected this to gemstones. She is connecting it now.
players.roleInGame: You are Sana's emotional shield but you are also a vulnerability. Defend Sana in early conversations. If someone presses you specifically about Sana's collecting habits, your honest answer damages Sana's case significantly.
players.witnessMemory: Was at the far end of the room from 8:40 to around 8:50. Did not see Sana anywhere near the display alcove.

players.name: Karishma
players.title: The Case Was Moved
players.whoYouAre: Karishma arrived early and has spent most of the evening watching the room rather than talking. She is perceptive and detail-oriented. She noticed something near the display case she initially dismissed as unimportant.
players.tonight: She has been mentally revisiting what she saw and is now certain it was significant.
players.alibi: Near the back of the room. She did not see Sana near the display area during the 8:40 to 8:50 window.
players.secret: Around 8:25 PM the Sapphire display case was at a slightly different angle than it had been at the start of the evening. A small repositioning -- the kind you only notice if you have been looking at it carefully for a while. She said nothing because she assumed a staff member had adjusted it. She knows now that was not what happened.
players.roleInGame: The display repositioning at 8:25 is critical. It means someone was physically at the case -- testing the bypass device or confirming its position -- more than fifteen minutes before the theft. Pair this with Akshaj's scratch detail. Share it at Meeting 2 for maximum impact.
players.witnessMemory: Around 8:40 she noticed someone moving quickly through the room toward the back. Dark formal outfit. Could not identify them.

players.name: Saachi
players.title: The Three Visits
players.whoYouAre: Saachi was genuinely excited about seeing the Sapphire and spent more time near the display case than almost anyone else tonight. Because of that, she noticed a pattern in Sana's behavior that nobody else caught.
players.tonight: She is now realizing that what she observed was not enthusiasm about the gem -- it was surveillance.
players.alibi: Near the couch area in the middle of the room from around 8:42 to 8:52.
players.secret: She counted three separate visits Sana made to the display case across the evening: at approximately 8:20, again around 8:35, and once more just before 8:40. Most guests visited once or twice. Sana kept returning, spending longer each time. After 8:40, Sana never came back to the case.
players.roleInGame: Three timed visits ending precisely when the theft window began is not casual interest. You are the only person who tracked this pattern. Be specific about the times when pressed. This is some of the strongest behavioral evidence in the game.
players.witnessMemory: Saw Sana at the display at 8:20, around 8:35, and just before 8:40. After 8:40 Sana was not near the display area.

players.name: Trishta
players.title: The 8:47 Alarm
players.whoYouAre: Trishta had set an 8:47 alarm on her watch entirely by coincidence for an unrelated reason. The alarm went off. Two to three seconds later she heard a muffled sound from the direction of the back storage room. She may have accidentally recorded the exact moment of the attack.
players.tonight: She is now trying to piece together whether what she heard at 8:47 was the attack on Dr. Mehta.
players.alibi: Near the bar area. Her watch alarm is timestamped proof of exactly where she was at 8:47.
players.secret: The muffled sound she heard at 8:47 came unmistakably from the direction of the storage room. She thought something had fallen. She did not see anyone moving toward the back in the moments before or after.
players.roleInGame: Your alarm locks the time of the attack at exactly 8:47. This is the timeline anchor the whole group needs. Combined with Somya's observation of the open storage door at 8:42, the attack window is confirmed: Sana was in the storage corridor between 8:42 and 8:47.
players.witnessMemory: Alarm went off at 8:47. Two to three seconds later -- a muffled sound from the storage room direction. Did not see Sana from 8:40 to 8:50.

players.name: Sana
players.title: The Composed Guest
players.whoYouAre: Sana has attended gala events for years and always knows how to move through a room without drawing attention. She arrived tonight with a plan she had been building for months. Tonight she executed it.
players.tonight: She has been calm, social, and deliberate. She has been socializing naturally and deflecting attention. She is aware that a few people noticed things. She is managing that now.
players.alibi: She will claim she was near the main group the entire evening and was talking to Trisha around 8:45. She was not. Trisha has no memory of this conversation.
players.secret: She stole the Sapphire. She disabled the display case at 8:40 using a bypass device, took the gem, placed it in the hidden compartment of her clutch bag, and struck Dr. Mehta when Dr. Mehta confronted her in the storage corridor. She has a flight to Vienna at 7 AM tomorrow. A buyer is waiting.
players.roleInGame: Appear shocked and cooperative. Ask questions. Help theorize. Cast doubt on anyone with technical knowledge -- suggest the theft required expertise no one here would have. If pressed on your alibi, hold firm on Trisha. If Trisha denies it, say she must be misremembering. Do not crack.
players.witnessMemory: STATED MEMORY (false): Was in the main area all evening. Was talking to Trisha around 8:45. Did not go near the display after 8:40. Has no idea what happened. -- This directly contradicts Somya, Krish, Saachi, Trishta, and Trisha's own account.

players.name: Somya
players.title: The Open Door
players.whoYouAre: Somya has been drifting between groups all evening and passed through almost every part of the room. Because she was never anchored to one spot, she noticed something that more stationary guests missed completely.
players.tonight: She walked past the storage hallway around 8:42 and saw something that did not register as significant until right now.
players.alibi: Moving between the couch area and the drinks table from around 8:45 to 8:52.
players.secret: The storage room door was ajar at 8:42 -- open by a few inches when it should have been fully closed. She glanced in briefly, saw nothing obvious, and kept walking. She assumed someone was using a back area.
players.roleInGame: An open storage door at 8:42 is the earliest physical confirmation that someone had already entered that corridor before the alarm was raised. This places Sana's movements in the storage area starting at 8:42 -- five full minutes before the official alarm. Share this the moment timeline questions come up.
players.witnessMemory: Around 8:42 the storage room door was open by a few inches. She looked in briefly, saw nothing, and kept walking.

players.name: Trisha
players.title: The Alibi That Doesn't Exist
players.whoYouAre: Trisha was near the bar for most of the evening and has a clear memory of who she spoke to and when. She is about to become a critical figure in this investigation without having done anything at all.
players.tonight: Sana plans to name Trisha as her alibi for the 8:40 to 8:50 window -- specifically claiming they were talking at around 8:45. That conversation did not happen.
players.alibi: Near the bar area the entire evening. She was there from 8:35 to past 8:55.
players.secret: Sana is using Trisha as a false alibi. If anyone asks Trisha directly whether she spoke with Sana between 8:40 and 8:50, her completely honest answer is: no, she does not remember that conversation. It did not happen.
players.roleInGame: You are the pin that pops Sana's entire alibi. When Sana's claimed alibi comes up in conversation, you must be completely honest: you do not remember speaking with Sana in that window. You were at the bar and it did not happen. This single fact collapses Sana's only defense.
players.witnessMemory: Was near the bar from 8:35 to past 8:55. Has no memory of speaking with Sana between 8:40 and 8:50. That conversation did not occur.

players.name: Hitayu
players.title: The Collector World Expert
players.whoYouAre: Hitayu has background knowledge in rare collectibles and understands the private market for artifacts like the Sapphire. When the theft was announced, his immediate thought was: this was not spontaneous.
players.tonight: He has been thinking through the logistics of the theft and what it would take to successfully move a stone of this value.
players.alibi: With Samaksh near the bar area from 8:35 to past 8:50.
players.secret: Selling a stolen artifact of this value on the private market requires an established buyer network built over years -- not weeks. The kind of contact who operates internationally, discreetly, and at this price point. Whoever did this has been operating in collector circles for a long time.
players.roleInGame: Your expertise reframes the entire investigation. Help the group understand this was a professional operation that required months of planning and existing contacts. This narrows the suspect list considerably -- and when the Orlov contact card surfaces it connects directly to what you know.
players.witnessMemory: Was with Samaksh from 8:35 to past 8:50. Did not see Sana in their vicinity during that entire window.

players.name: Samaksh
players.title: The Rehearsed Story
players.whoYouAre: Samaksh is observant and analytical. He has spent most of the evening quietly reading the room and was one of the first people to notice that something about Sana's account of her own evening feels slightly wrong.
players.tonight: He has been building a mental timeline and something about how precisely Sana described her movements struck him as practiced rather than recalled.
players.secret: When Sana described where she was during the theft, she used unusually specific details -- the kind of precision that comes from rehearsing a story, not from genuinely trying to remember an evening. Real memories are vague and slightly inconsistent. Hers were polished.
players.roleInGame: An over-rehearsed alibi is a real behavioral red flag. Raise this observation to push the group to press Sana harder on exact details. Also: you and Hitayu both clearly noticed Sana's absence from the main group between 8:40 and 8:50 -- your combined testimony on that gap is important.
players.witnessMemory: Was with Hitayu all evening. Clearly noticed Sana was absent from the main group from roughly 8:40 to 8:50. Remembers the gap because he looked for her at one point and could not find her.

players.name: Krish
players.title: The Bag Adjustment
players.whoYouAre: Krish is naturally observant without being nosy. He registers small behavioral details and then files them away without meaning to. Tonight one of those details is suddenly very important.
players.tonight: He saw Sana leave the main group around 8:40 and return around 8:50 with a specific gesture he almost did not notice at the time.
players.alibi: Moving between the couch area and the center of the room.
players.secret: Sana was gone from the main group from approximately 8:40 to 8:50. When she came back she looked slightly flushed and made a very deliberate adjustment to her clutch bag -- closing it and pressing it against her side -- before composing herself and re-entering a conversation. He noticed the bag adjustment specifically because it looked intentional.
players.roleInGame: The ten-minute absence plus the deliberate bag adjustment is the clearest behavioral evidence directly linking Sana to the moment she placed the Sapphire into the hidden compartment. You are the only person who witnessed both the departure and the return. Be specific when asked.
players.witnessMemory: Saw Sana leave the main area around 8:40 and return around 8:50. When she came back she deliberately adjusted her clutch bag and then composed herself before rejoining a group.

players.name: Akshaj
players.title: The Scratch on the Lock
players.whoYouAre: Akshaj has a background in security systems and when he arrived at the gala he examined the display case out of professional habit. What he noticed may be the earliest physical evidence of tampering in the entire investigation.
players.tonight: He looked at the display case within the first fifteen minutes of arriving and registered something he did not act on at the time.
players.alibi: Moving between the back area and the main room around 8:45 to 8:50.
players.secret: When he examined the display case near the start of the evening, he noticed a faint but very fresh scratch near the lock mechanism -- the kind of mark left when a device is inserted and removed carefully. The scratch had clean edges, meaning it was recent. He said nothing because he assumed it was from installation.
players.roleInGame: A fresh scratch on the lock near the start of the evening means the bypass device was tested or inserted before most guests arrived. This is the earliest evidence of premeditation in the whole case. Pair this with Karishma's observation about the repositioned case at 8:25 -- together they confirm Sana was working on the case long before 8:40.
players.witnessMemory: Examined the display case near the start of the evening. Noticed a fresh scratch near the lock. Did not see Sana near his area between 8:40 and 8:50.

privateAddons.name: Karishma
privateAddons.power: You are a SECRET DETECTIVE. Do not reveal this to anyone. Your vote at the final accusation counts DOUBLE -- the hosts apply this silently without announcing it.
privateAddons.mission: Solve the theft and guide the group toward the truth without exposing that you have a special role.
privateAddons.seed: You are suspicious of Samaksh. He is very analytical and has been steering conversations in particular directions all night. Is he building a genuine case -- or controlling what the group thinks?
privateAddons.strategy: Press Samaksh on what exactly he claims to have observed and when. Watch if he deflects or redirects. But do not get so fixed on him that you miss the actual evidence. Follow the clues, not the personality.

privateAddons.name: Samaksh
privateAddons.power: You are a SECRET DETECTIVE. Do not reveal this to anyone. Your vote at the final accusation counts DOUBLE -- the hosts apply this silently without announcing it.
privateAddons.mission: Solve the theft and guide the group toward the truth without exposing that you have a special role.
privateAddons.seed: You are suspicious of Karishma. She arrived early, says she noticed the display was repositioned, and her alibi puts her near the back of the room -- close to the storage corridor. Is she a sharp observer -- or a careful deflector?
privateAddons.strategy: Ask Karishma for precise details about what she saw and exactly when. If her account shifts, flag it. But do not get tunnel vision -- if the evidence points clearly elsewhere, follow it.

privateAddons.name: Sana
privateAddons.whatReallyHappened: You stole the Sapphire of Shadows. You have been a private gemstone collector for years, operating through channels your social circle knows nothing about. You learned about the Sapphire six months ago, registered for this gala early, obtained scan data three weeks in advance, sourced a custom electronic bypass device, and tonight you executed the plan. You disabled the case at 8:40, took the Sapphire, placed it in the hidden compartment of your clutch bag, and struck Dr. Mehta when she confronted you in the storage corridor. You have a flight to Vienna at 7 AM tomorrow. A buyer named V. Orlov is waiting.
privateAddons.publicApproach: You were in the main area all evening. Around 8:45 you were talking to Trisha near the bar. You did not go near the display case after 8:40. You had absolutely no idea any of this would happen.
privateAddons.cracks: 1. Trisha has no memory of talking to you at 8:45 -- the alibi collapses under direct questioning. 2. Krish saw you leave the main area at 8:40 and return at 8:50 adjusting your bag. 3. Samaksh and Hitayu also noticed you were absent during that window. 4. Saachi counted three visits you made to the display case before 8:40. 5. Shubham overheard you say 'it's ready' on a phone call and you ended it suddenly. 6. Shubhi knows about your collecting habits if pressed. 7. Eshika remembers you asked her about gemstone authentication three months ago.
privateAddons.deflect: Suggest the theft required technical expertise that nobody here would have. Lean into the forensics note -- 'a custom bypass device? Who here even knows what that is?' Cast vague doubt toward Akshaj since he has a security background. Do not name anyone directly -- just plant the idea.
privateAddons.emotionalCover: Appear shocked and upset. Ask questions. Help theorize. Seem like you are on the group's side. The more you appear to be solving the crime, the less anyone looks at you.
